2011-01-01.title: A Kurdish Rebel Softens His Tone for Skeptical Ears

2011-01-27.title: Egyptian Youths Drive the Revolt Against Mubarak
2011-01-27.abstract: Hosni Mubarak’s traditional opponents have been displaced by the leaderless tens of thousands of young Egyptians who turned out this week to demand an end to his 30-year rule.

2011-01-28.title: In City’s Teeming Heart, a Place to Gaze and Graze
2011-01-28.abstract: To the uninitiated eye, the Maidan is just a park. To Kolkata, it is a place to recharge the soul.

2011-01-28.title: Third Day of Protests in Egypt
2011-01-28.abstract: Mohamed ElBaradei, the Nobel laureate who has become a leading opponent of President Hosni Mubarak of Egypt, returned to Cairo in an attempt to galvanize youth-led street protests that extended into a third day across the country.

2011-01-28.title: Cables Show Delicate U.S. Dealings With Egypt’s Leaders
2011-01-28.abstract: A trove of dispatches made public by the anti-secrecy group WikiLeaks paint a vivid picture of the delicate dealings between the United States and Egypt.

2011-01-28.title: Seizing a Moment, Al Jazeera Galvanizes Arab Frustration
2011-01-28.abstract: The channel has helped shape a narrative of popular rage against oppressive American-backed Arab governments.

2011-01-28.title: Waves of Unrest Spread to Yemen, Shaking a Region
2011-01-28.abstract: Thousands protested in Yemen and secular and Islamist Egyptian opposition leaders vowed to join rallies as calls for change rang across the Arab world.

2011-01-29.title: A Nobelist Has an Unfamiliar Role in Protests
2011-01-29.abstract: A police confrontation is the latest turn in Mohamed ElBaradei’s unexpected second career in Egypt’s politics.

2011-01-29.title: Al Jazeera Covers Protests Despite Hurdles
2011-01-29.abstract: The channel has provided exhaustive coverage of Egypt’s protests despite repeated efforts to block its broadcasts.

2011-01-29.title: Willi Dansgaard Dies at 88; Read Climates in Old Ice
2011-01-29.abstract: Drilling into the Greenland icecap, Mr. Dansgaard was able to analyze ancient bubbles of trapped oxygen and gauge temperature.

2011-01-29.title: France: Gay Marriage Ban Upheld
2011-01-29.abstract: The constitutional council upheld longstanding legislation on Friday that effectively bans marriage for same-sex couples.

2011-01-29.title: Egyptian Hopes Converged in Fight for Cairo Bridge
2011-01-29.abstract: Hours of struggle for a bridge defined Friday’s events as people shed fears of the government, a few steps at a time.

2011-01-30.title: Choice of Suleiman Likely to Please the Military, Not the Crowds
2011-01-30.abstract: Omar Suleiman, President Hosni Mubarak’s choice for vice president of Egypt, is the establishment’s candidate.

2011-01-30.title: Obama Presses for Change but Not a New Face at the Top
2011-01-30.abstract: Officials said concern about a potential power vacuum drove President Obama’s decision not to call for Mr. Mubarak’s resignation.

2011-01-30.title: For Americans Stranded in Egypt, No Quick Exits
2011-01-30.abstract: Thousands of people were trapped in hotels or the airport, and there was no indication when they might leave.

2011-01-30.title: Cairo: After Curfew
2011-01-30.abstract: Protesters ignored a nighttime curfew on Saturday and carried on after President Hosni Mubarak of Egypt appointed the intelligence chief, Omar Suleiman, as vice president.

2011-01-30.title: New Chants in Cairo
2011-01-30.abstract: Protesters shouted a new chant after President Hosni Mubarak of Egypt appointed the intelligence chief, Omar Suleiman, as his vice president.

2011-01-31.title: Tourists Trapped in Egypt
2011-01-31.abstract: As thousands of foreign tourists and Egyptians try to leave the country, reactions range from nervous to stoic.

2011-01-31.title: Protest’s Old Guard Falls In Behind the Young
2011-01-31.abstract: Political organizers, many younger than 30, are taking the lead in efforts to topple a regime older than they are.

2011-01-31.title: Myanmar Opens Parliament for First Time Since the ’80s
2011-01-31.abstract: The move will effectively dissolve the country’s junta, but the military will control most of the seats in the two-chamber legislature.

2011-01-31.title: Losses at Afghan Bank Could Be $900 Million
2011-01-31.abstract: Fraud and mismanagement have resulted in enormous losses, heightening concerns about wider repercussions.

2011-01-31.title: Political Crisis Starts to Be Felt Economically
2011-01-31.abstract: Shortages of fuel, rising prices for food and a slowdown of many kinds of businesses are increasing the sense of crisis.
